content.problemStatement:
You are given a connected graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and N‚ąí1N - 1N‚ąí1 bidirectional edges. Also, you are given a sequence K1,K2,‚Ä¶,KNK_1, K_2, \ldots, K_NK1‚Äč,K2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,KN‚Äč.

Let's denote the distance between vertices uuu and vvv by d(u,v)d(u, v)d(u,v). Next, for each valid iii, let's define DiD_iDi‚Äč as the maximum integer such that there are at least KiK_iKi‚Äč vertices vvv with d(i,v)>Did(i, v) \gt D_id(i,v)>Di‚Äč. (It can be proven that such integers exist for the given constraints.)

Your task is to find the values of D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DND_1, D_2, \ldots, D_ND1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äč.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ki‚Č§N‚ąí11 \le K_i \le N - 11‚Č§Ki‚Äč‚Č§N‚ąí1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers K1,K2,‚Ä¶,KNK_1, K_2, \ldots, K_NK1‚Äč,K2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,KN‚Äč.
Each of the next N‚ąí1N - 1N‚ąí1 lines contains two space-separated integeres uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DND_1, D_2, \ldots, D_ND1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DN‚Äč.
